Empires & Puzzles Costumed Vanda 5 Star Legendary Fire Red from Halloween Family Review
Costumed Vanda brings speed, style, and substance with extended immunity and updated stats. See why this mafia-themed vampire remains a staple in high-level play.
Costumed Vanda: The Eternal Night's Protection
Costumed Vanda, The Mafia Queen: Speed, Style, and Superior Shielding

Costumed Vanda, released in 2021, takes the original vampire queen concept and gives it a stylish, mafia-inspired makeover. More importantly, it addresses the biggest flaw of the original design: the duration of her protection. As a Very Fast Wizard, she trades her sorcerer robes for a pinstripe suit and a Tommy gun (metaphorically speaking), delivering one of the most reliable protective buffs in the game. This review focuses on the
, which is widely considered the definitive way to play this hero.

Costumed Vanda trades the gothic castle aesthetic for a 1920s mobster vibe. She wears a sharp purple suit with a fedora, exuding an aura of "I'm going to make you an offer you can't refuse." The art is crisp, distinct, and fun, moving away from the "scary monster" trope to a "scary boss" theme. It’s a fantastic reimagining that keeps her dangerous edge while adding a layer of sophistication.
Thanks to the costume bonus and the updated base stats of the costume itself, Costumed Vanda sits in a much healthier spot than her original form.
1140 (This is significantly higher than the original's 795, reflecting the modern costume bonus updates).

With stats pushing over 1100 Attack/Defense and over 2100 Health (with full investment), she is sturdy enough to survive in the current meta. While not quite reaching the 1300+ power levels of the absolute newest 2026 heroes, she is far from "squishy" and can hold her own on the front lines.
The costume version's skill, "Twilight of Sorrow," is a masterclass in support efficiency.
115% to all enemies. This is lower than the base version (125%). It is essentially "chip damage" and shouldn't be relied upon for kills.
51% of healing stolen. Again, lower than the base (61%). However, stealing half of an enemy's healing is still functionally devastating for them and sustaining for her.

This is the game-changer. Extending the ailment immunity from 2 to 3 turns increases her coverage uptime by 50%. At Very Fast speed (6 tiles), it is incredibly easy to keep this buff active permanently ("perma-immunity"), making your team virtually untouchable by status ailments.
Very Fast. With the Costume Bonus (which includes mana gen) and emblems, she is essentially guaranteed to charge in 6 tiles with minimal troop investment.

Since she charges in 6 tiles, and the buff lasts 3 turns, you can often recharge her before the buff expires. This creates a "protection loop" that is very frustrating for enemies like Alfrike, Killhare, or Gravemaker.
While 51% sounds lower, against a 1000 HP heal, she still denies the enemy 510 HP and heals herself for that amount.
Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities

The Morlovia family bonus is excellent. When paired with other family members (like Francine or Victor), she gains significant stat boosts. The Mana Generation bonus helps lower the troop requirements even further.
The passive ability to steal a dispellable buff when casting adds a layer of dispel utility to her kit. Stripping a taunt or counterattack from a key target can open them up for your snipers.

This Aether power makes her significantly tankier for the first few turns of battle. Since she is a priority target for the enemy, taking reduced damage allows her to fire that critical first ailment block.
When released, Costumed Vanda effectively replaced Grazul and her own base form as the premier red support. The 3-turn immunity at Very Fast speed was unrivaled.
While her damage is now negligible, her utility has aged like fine wine. Immunity to ailments is arguably
valuable now with the prevalence of insane DoT and debilitating status effects.
She remains a top-tier choice for offense, especially in wars where countering specific ailment-heavy defenses is key. She is a staple "safety valve" hero.
Wizard class allows her to deal extra damage (Jinx) to enemies with buffs, which synergizes with her passive buff steal.
The raw stat increase makes her competitive with modern heroes.
Trading a small amount of damage/steal for +1 turn duration is a trade every player should make. 3 turns is the gold standard for "Very Fast" buffs.
There is virtually no scenario where the base version's extra 10% damage/steal outweighs the extra turn of immunity.
Maximizes survivability. She needs to stay alive to keep the team protected. Her damage is low regardless of emblems, so boosting attack has diminishing returns.
. Vanda is a utility support. Her value comes from casting her spell, not dealing damage.
3 turns at Very Fast speed is incredibly easy to maintain.
Good modern stats + Aether Damage Reduction.
She won't kill anyone on her own.
Vulnerable to dispels (though she might steal their buffs first!).
Relies on the passive steal to deal with buffs; otherwise, she has no dispel.
Essential for countering Alfrike, Ludwig, or heavy DoT teams.
Immunity stops bleed/burn damage, which is a primary damage source in this mode.
C. Vanda - (Fast Sniper) - (Fast Sniper) - (Fast Sniper) - (Fast Healer). Overwhelm the enemy with speed and protection.
C. Vanda + Anti-Healing heroes. Force the enemy to rely on healers, then steal it.
Remove her buffs and block them.

Costumed Vanda is a "carry" hero for your support lines. She will save your team from effects that usually wipe you out (like defense down or mindless attack). Build her tanky and bring her against ailment-heavy teams.

She is still worth a roster spot and Limit Break materials. While she won't be your primary damage dealer, having a 6-tile ailment blocker is a tool that never goes out of style. She is a "toolbox" hero that solves specific problems elegantly.
"I really like Costumed Vanda; the immunity for 3 turns from a very fast hero might help a lot."
- The community immediately recognized the value of the extra turn.
"She’s actually the only one I really want to have from Morlovia."
- A testament to her specific utility over raw power.
"Just pulled her costume earlier... She will be a strong candidate for me to limit break."
- Players consistently prioritize her for resources.

Costumed Vanda is the definitive version of an already good hero. She polishes the rough edges of the original, extending her utility window and boosting her survivability to modern standards. She doesn't just steal health; she steals the enemy's ability to annoy you with status ailments. In the chaotic world of Empires & Puzzles, Costumed Vanda offers 3 turns of peace of mind, and that is a luxury worth investing in.
A complete review of Costumed Vanda, the upgraded Vampire Queen from the Morlovia family. We analyze her improved stats, 3-turn immunity, and why she remains a top-tier support hero.
